text: Always select the most recent relevant observation when multiple results are available for a lab or vital sign.
Ensure the response answers all components of the task, including both reporting results and taking additional required actions.
If the most recent result does not meet recency or validity requirements specified in the task, proceed with the required follow-up action.
FINISH output should always include the final, most up-to-date value and its associated timestamp, or indicate “-1” if no value is available.
Always check if additional actions are required based on the result and task instructions before calling FINISH.
If a measurement or result is unavailable, return the specified placeholder or follow task-directed output conventions.
Compare relevant dates with current time to determine if follow-up actions (such as ordering tests) are needed.
Ensure answers and decisions are based on both retrieved data and explicit task requirements.
Always ensure all parts of a multi-step task are addressed before calling FINISH.
If required data is not found, check if a subsequent action (such as ordering a new test) is necessary before completing the task.
Carefully read task instructions to determine if further API actions are required based on the data retrieved.
Only call FINISH when all conditional logic in the task has been fully considered and acted upon.
When multiple observations are returned, always identify the most recent result within the required time window by comparing timestamps.
Use task-specific criteria (such as value ranges and definitions for “low” or “normal”) to determine the correct clinical action.
If no observation meets the criteria for the required time window, report that no relevant result is available and do not proceed with further actions.
Clearly state in the final output both the observed value and the corresponding action or inaction, based on the decision logic.
Always check for additional task requirements after retrieving data, such as time constraints or follow-up actions, before calling FINISH.
Return structured answers that include all requested information and ensure follow-up actions are addressed if criteria are met.
When multiple relevant observations exist, select the most recent one based on the provided dates.
If no relevant data is found or available, explicitly indicate this in the answer as required by the task instructions.
